title: Trichotosia bracteata
taxonomy: Trichotosia bracteata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. Trichotosia bracteata is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes many other orchid species. The genus Trichotosia is a relatively small group of orchids, with about 30 species found in the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The classification of Trichotosia bracteata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Trichotosia bracteata is a species of orchid, and its medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, various species within the Trichotosia genus have been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine. In some regions, Trichotosia species are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
